stories.headline: PA House passes HB 2233 unanimously, requiring utilities to study advanced transmission tech
stories.summary: The Pennsylvania House on Monday passed HB 2233 by a unanimous vote, letting the PUC order utilities and other transmission owners to add advanced transmission technologies — high-performance conductors, dynamic line ratings, advanced power flow controllers, topology optimization — to upgrade or new transmission projects.

stories.headline: PA Turnpike's Allegheny Mountain bypass plan draws Somerset County opposition
stories.summary: A Turnpike Commission proposal to reroute the cross-state highway by blasting a 1,000-foot-wide cut through Allegheny Mountain in Somerset County — Somerset REC service territory — faces opposition from local leaders who say it would scar a pristine wilderness corridor.

stories.headline: Rabb votes against PA sex-trafficking bills two weeks before PA-3 primary
stories.summary: State Rep. Chris Rabb, in a tight three-way Democratic primary for Philadelphia's 3rd Congressional seat, voted Monday against two PA House bills increasing penalties for child sexual abuse and trafficking, saying the measures wouldn't achieve their stated purposes — a vote his primary opponents are already using.

stories.headline: NERC issues rare Level 3 alert mandating action on data center load losses
stories.summary: NERC issued its highest-tier Level 3 alert May 4 with seven required actions for transmission planners, operators and balancing authorities after multiple events where 1+ GW of data center load disconnected in seconds; entities must acknowledge by May 11 and respond by Aug. 3.

stories.headline: Caterpillar to triple power generation capacity, raises 2030 targets on AI load growth
stories.summary: Caterpillar said it will triple its power generation manufacturing capacity and raised its 2030 financial targets, citing data center and AI-driven backup and prime power demand — the latest equipment-side signal that PJM-area gas turbine and reciprocating engine supply remains tight.

stories.headline: California subpoenas Golden State Wind over Trump offshore lease deal
stories.summary: California's AG subpoenaed Golden State Wind, the lessee of one of the state's three federal offshore wind areas, seeking documents on its dealings with the Trump administration after the developer reached a private deal Albany officials say bypassed state permitting.

stories.headline: Trump-backed challengers oust at least 5 of 7 Indiana state senators in redistricting revenge
stories.summary: Trump-aligned PACs spent roughly $8M and defeated at least five of seven targeted Indiana GOP state senators — including Senate caucus chair Travis Holdman — punishing those who blocked his 2025 mid-decade redistricting demand and signaling South Carolina, Tennessee, Louisiana and Alabama legislators eyeing the same fight.

stories.headline: Trump pauses 'Project Freedom' Strait of Hormuz mission, citing Iran agreement progress
stories.summary: Trump abruptly suspended the U.S. effort to escort civilian shipping through the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, saying talks with Iran could yield a finalized agreement; the Navy blockade of Iranian ports continues, and oil prices eased on the announcement.

stories.headline: Senate Judiciary moves $72B reconciliation markup toward May 19; floor week of May 18
stories.summary: Chair Chuck Grassley placed the $72B ICE/CBP/DHS reconciliation text on Senate Judiciary's agenda for May 19, with HSGAC's Rand Paul expected to mark up his portion this month; GOP leaders aim to bring the package to the Senate floor the week of May 18.

stories.headline: Pirates blanked 9-0 by Diamondbacks as Eduardo Rodriguez tosses 7 shutout innings
stories.summary: Eduardo Rodriguez allowed two hits over seven innings with seven strikeouts; Geraldo Perdomo and Gabriel Moreno each doubled in Arizona's five-run sixth as the Pirates (19-17) dropped the series opener 9-0, their largest deficit of the season; Paul Skenes (4-2, 3.18) goes tonight.

stories.headline: Malkin's camp setting Dubas meeting this week to discuss Penguins future
stories.summary: Per The Athletic's Josh Yohe, Malkin's representatives are reaching out to GM Kyle Dubas this week to lock down an in-person meeting on a new contract; Malkin (61 points in 56 games) becomes a UFA July 1 and said at exit interviews he wants one more NHL year, in Pittsburgh or elsewhere.
